creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer channel announces a World's Fair event in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. The announcement includes ticketing and scheduling information available at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin from Together AI presents a stack of techniques for training LLMs with extremely long context windows (3-5 million tokens), including fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. The novel Untied Ulysses approach further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers across iterations, achieving 25% longer sequences than prior Ulysses implementations.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Nupur Sharma from Qodo describes the U-curve phenomenon where agents attending to context window start and end while dropping middle content. The talk covers iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as solutions, plus an orchestration approach splitting reasoning models for discovery with lighter deterministic models for validation.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carrie, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Cloudflare's Durable Objects serve as a compute unit for AI agents with addressable, persistent, stateful properties and sub-15ms latency. The Agents SDK builds on this with resumable streaming, multi-tab sync, and background scheduling capabilities for agent deployment without distributed systems engineering.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A stream on Saturday where he will discuss AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon while playing chess. The stream is part of regular Saturday and Wednesday content releases for the Dynamous community.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a multi-model workflow that routes planning and reasoning tasks to Claude Opus and visual design tasks to Gemini 3.5 Flash. The approach leverages each model's strengths to build full-stack applications with better aesthetic output at lower cost than using a single flagship model.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a multi-provider AI workflow combining Claude Opus for planning and integration, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design, and automated deployment. The take is that Gemini excels at frontend design while Opus handles planning and integration better, creating a fast pipeline for beautiful frontends.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building and optimizing the wrapper layer around coding agents, comprising rules, skills, hooks, and subagents at the session level, and orchestrating multiple agent sessions into workflows. It emphasizes that the harness, not the model, accounts for roughly 98% of a production coding agent's value.
